key_insight: A feedforward neural network using the writhe density matrix can classify the first six prime two-component links with 97% accuracy, showing that topology-aware geometric features capture link type effectively.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast, data-driven alternative to expensive exact topological calculations for identifying link structures in polymer, DNA, and protein systems.
method: Computed writhe density matrices from thermally equilibrated link configurations
Trained a feedforward neural network on the first six prime links
Tested robustness across temperature, component length, and Gaussian noise
evidence_signal: The model achieved 97% accuracy on thermally equilibrated configurations, stayed strong across temperatures and link lengths, and degraded sharply when topology-altering Gaussian noise was added.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated for two-component links only, so extension to more complex multi-component topologies may require retraining and careful validation.
paper_title: Writhe-Based Polymer Link Classification Using Machine Learning
